text.heading: REPORT OF THE MAY 08. 2003 FGN/XXX DIALOGUE COMMITTEE MEETING
1. Preamble
text.p: In the last report on the state of the work of the Technical Committee (TC) which was constituted by the FGN/ Dialogue Committee, certain problems that hampered the work of the Technical Committee were identified. Also, certain areas in which no consensus had been reached and/or where agreement could not be reached on how to record the differences of positions of the two teams, Government and , were indicated. The Technical Committee therefore adjourned to enable the members of both the Government Team and the Team to consult, to allow time for reflection on how to proceed, and also to enable the Chairman of the Technical Committee, , brief as well as consult the Chairman of the Dialogue Committee, , the Honourable Minister of Labour, Employment and Productivity. The TC also agreed to resume its work on May 06, 2003, if the Chairman of the Dialogue Committee considered it useful to have the TC to proceed with, and complete its work. The decision of the TC to leave to the Chairman of the Dialogue Committee the issue of whether or not it should continue and complete its work was informed by the Government Team's insistence that the TC had come to an unbreakable deadlock and could make no further progress, especially on one of the key issues, namely, the scheduling of the specific amounts in the Agreement and the related issue of the specific amount to be provided to make the universities functional when academic work resumes. In contrast, Team was optimistic that a way could be found to resolve the impasse.
text.heading: 2. Meeting of the Technical Committee with the Minister of Labour
text.p: As it turned out, the Honourable Minister of Labour, Employment and Productivity , by his letter of April 30, 2003, invited the Chairman and members of the TC to a meeting with him at 9.00 a.m. on May 06, 2003. He also summoned a meeting of the Dialogue Committee for May 07, 2003 at 4.00 p.m. The special meeting of TC was presided over by the Honourable Minister of Labour , Employment and Productivity. It was attended by all the members of the Team, plus all the ASUU Teams Technical advisers; the Chairman of the TC, , and his two Technical Advisers, Her Excellency Mrs and , Chairman of the Committee of Vice-chancellors. In attendance also were the Permanent Secretary and Director in charge of Trade Union Matters, Federal Ministry of Labour; the Executive Secretary of NUC, as well as the Secretariat staff of the TC. The Government Team in the meeting had two of its three members, as well as, one of the technical advisers to the team, namely, the one drawn from the Federal Ministry of Finance.

It was at this special meeting of the TC, presided over by the Honourable Minister of Labour, that the Team made a proposal which broke the impasse on re-scheduling of the specific amounts of funds required by the universities as contained in FGN/ Agreement of June 30, 2001. The Team proposed that the following clause should be inserted in the report of the TC:

"that, in the event of the Federal Government having any difficulties in meeting its obligations as contained in the schedule of funds to be provided to universities in any year, should be invited to enable the two sides work out mutually acceptable solutions". The Government Team then agreed to provide a schedule of the specific amounts of required funds as contained in the June 2001 FGN/ Agreement the understanding that the clause would be inserted into the report".

With this positive development, the TC withdrew from the meeting and then resumed its normal meeting promising the Chairman of the Dialogue Committee and the Honourable Minister of Labour that it would finish its work and submit a report by 4.00 pm on May, 7 2003 to enable the Dialogue Committee hold its meeting as already planned
text.heading: 2. The Resumed Meeting of the Technical Committee
text.p: The leader of the Government Team had, at the meeting with the Honourable Minister of Labour and Chairman Dialogue Committee which ended at about 11.00 am on May, 6 2003, requested for a two-hour interval. This was to enable him consult his "principals" and prepare a schedule acceptable to them before presentation to the TC. His request was granted and the TC therefore agreed to resume by 1.00 pm on the same day.
When the TC resumed at 2.00 p.m. on May 6 2003, with its work, which was mainly to approve a draft report distributed to members a week earlier, it was delayed by one strange development.

• The Government Team produced what it titled "schedule of required funds" and defined "required funds" in the FGN/ Agreement to imply that the Federal Government was not under any obligation to fund Federal universities in line with the amount specified in the Agreement. Rather, the Federal Government would provide whatever amount it wished or could afford, and the rest would be provided by the private sector, parents, civil society organisations, etc.

• Also in the paper on Schedule of Required Funds presented by the Government Team was inserted, after the schedule, a clause totally different from the one mutually agreed upon in the meeting with Honourable Minister of Labour and Productivity.

The two clauses contained in the paper on scheduling by the Government Team, which tended to contradict or undermine the provisions on funding in Chapter Five of the FGN/ Agreement of 2001, are reproduced below for ease of reference:
Clause (b) on page 1 of Government Team's paper
"From the above provisions the (Government) Team notes as follows:

(b) that the levels of funds indicated under 5.1.1 a Recurrent Expenditure (2001-2003), 5.1.1. b Capital Expenditure (2001-2003) represent a summation of the required funds by the universities, which come from all sources including Government, private sector, internally generated funds, funds expended by parents in the maintenance of their children, etc. It was therefore not intended to come from Government exclusively.
Another clause on page 3 of Government Team's paper

In the case that in any year, Government is unable to achieve the desired quantum of funding as scheduled, the Executive Secretary, on behalf of the Honourable Minister of Education, shall invite and explain to them what efforts have been made and the inability of Government to meet the scheduled funds for the year. shall understand and shall not use this as a basis fo a strike."

The Chairman of TC, , urged the leader of the Governemnt Team to modify the first clause so as not to create the impression that Governemnt was interpreting the FGN/ Agreement in a manner that freed Government of any specific financial obligations to the universities. The Chairman fo CVC urged the Government Team to remove that clause altogether since it made the schedule of required funds prepared by the Government Team totally meaningless. team also asked the Government Team to delete the first clause and modify the second one in the manner already mutually agreed upon in the meeting with Honourable Minister of Labour. The Government Team rejected the pleas and requests on the ground that it could not, on its own, alter a document prepared with the consent and approval of its principals. When it became obvious that, once more, a deadlock had been reached on this point, the TC decided to adjourn its meeting and resume the following day.
To facilitate the work of the TC, the Chairman proposed, and the two sides agreed on the following.

• First, a sub-committee made up of two members from each team, that is, (i.e. four members) including the team leader in each side, should meet early the following day and agree on the text of the report, including the aspect of it that reflected Government's position on scheduling of funds.
• Second, the sub-committee should also reflect the amendments to the draft report earlier agreed upon by the TC.
• Third, the financial implications or all aspects of the Agreement, as determined by another sub-committee, set up earlier, should be inserted in the text of the report.

• The TC, in the hope that much of the work would be done at the sub-committee level, therefore, decided to reconvene at 1.00 p.m. on May 7 2003, to approve the report, sign it and submit it to the Minister of Labour. It was the hope of the TC that, between 1.00 p.m. and 4.00 p.m. (3hrs), it would be able to produce the final clean copy of its report.
The TC did not meet this target for the following reasons.
• First, the Government Team did not agree to modify its submission on schedule of required funds until about 2.00 p.m. thereafter, it was incorporated into the draft report.

• Second, the Government Team questioned the amount provided by the subcommittee on financial implications concerning the quantum of funds required to pay the approved salary (including the arrears). The Government Team put the amount required at less than N (actually N) while the sub-committee on financial implications put the figure at N (for the period, May 1, 2001 to December 2003). The Government Team's table did not specify the number of months. The matter was then referred to the TC for resolution.

• Third, and most important, on the one hand, all the members of the Team (including its Technical Advisers) were present and punctual; two members of the Government Team and one technical adviser were also present and punctual, , representing the Minister of Education, was however, conspicuously absent. Mrs , member of Board of Governors, was present and on time.

• On the other hand, the Chairman of the TC did not, contrary to his habit, come to the meeting in time. Indeed, he was not there until 3.35 p.m., about 25 minutes to the scheduled 4.00 p.m. time for the beginning of the meeting of the Dialogue Committee. The explanation he gave for his unusual lateness was that, despite his frantic calls from his hotel to the , no vehicle was provided until that late in the day. The leader of the Government Team, a Director in the , was fully aware of his position, he explained. It is on record that before the incident, the had on all occasions provided a vehicle for the Chairman of the committee for his movement to and from his hotel, punctually. It was only when the chairman of the TC arrived that the process of approving and producing the final, clean copy of the report was commenced. The report was ready by about 5.00 p.m.
